rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes, when referring to component ports.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE clauses to avoid type errors.
generic_rules: Ensure all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope or declared within the same annex.
Validate that range assumptions and guarantees reference concrete bounds or named constants.
Avoid using subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
When a validator reports type errors, trace unresolved identifiers and fix root causes rather than adding architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must reference only visible identifiers or helpers declared in the same annex.
Range guarantees should use named constants or visible feature names for bounds.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helpers in AGREE clauses.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
